Pass 1 #92.13 had this. The "Sarimu Grove" rendering is Whisper-mangled "Saipan" — the Northern Mariana Island. Cross-corpus WWII Pacific theater reference // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
Pass 1 #92.14 had this. Canonical WWII Pacific atoll where 1944 Marshall Islands campaign US Navy base was located. The "Henwoven" form is Whisper-mangled "Eniwetok"

pending_context.whisper: "I think it was Joe and Marshall"
pending_context.notes: Speaker says "We knew it was Joe and Marshall. It was a long way." Likely referring to Roi-Namur, the canonical northern atoll in Kwajalein where the US Navy had a major WWII supply base. "Joe and Marshall" is Whisper-degraded "Roi" + "Marshall (Islands)"

pending_context.whisper: "the sent-john" / "the sent-john was the same way"

pending_context.notes: Speaker references "the sent-john" multiple times in context of a paternalistic Claiborne County white figure. Could be a family name like "Sinjon" or "Saint-John"
Speaker references his older brother Calvin who worked with him. Whisper renders the name inconsistently

Speaker references "the blackhead group" — a local Port Gibson Black-youth group that "threw rocks at people, cars" during the desegregation crisis. Speaker says "they broke that group up" — referencing organized adult Movement leadership stopping youth violence. Local term, canonical first-person testimony
Speaker says his young son "wanted to be just like Bruce Sheer" (a member of the blackhead group). Could be "Bruce Shears" or a local youth militant. Worth research
Speaker says Ms. Crowder led songs at the youth choir during mass meetings — local Port Gibson Movement music leader. Canonical-from-context

pending_context.candidate_correction: (Local Port Gibson youth choir leader nickname)
pending_context.notes: Speaker references the youth choir leader by the local nickname "Dawg" — can't recall her real name. Worth flagging for the corpus
Speaker references "$16,000 in the bank" but the math doesn't work for a $1400 land purchase, sharecropper navy private 1944-46. Likely a Whisper digit-insertion. More plausibly "$1,600"
Speaker references "George and Wogan, I lit those students back around" — possible local helper at the 1966 Alcorn demonstration. Worth research

pending_context.whisper: "Mr. Mehaw's" / "He was the four out of $60"
pending_context.notes: Speaker confirms the seller name. Whisper renders "Mr. Mehaw" — possibly "Mr. McHaw" or "Mr. Meadows"

pending_context.whisper: "Just a quarter mile east of the poor, we are now."
pending_context.candidate_correction: Just a quarter mile east of where we are now."
pending_context.notes: Whisper inserts "poor" where the speaker probably says "where" or "road"; context is the speaker locating his birth-spot relative to his current home. Phonetic mishearing; meaning-preserving correction.

pending_context.whisper: "We were on the Facebook, we had a bunch of national and we had B-Slam."
pending_context.candidate_correction: We had two schools. We were on the back [path]
pending_context.notes: Severe Whisper degradation in the schools-discussion passage. "Facebook" is an obvious anachronism for a 1920s schools description; speaker is describing his two community schools. Cannot reliably reconstruct the original. Flag for adversarial review.

pending_context.whisper: "She lived by, she was young with me"
pending_context.candidate_correction: She lived nearby [or 'right by'], she was younger than me
pending_context.notes: Speaker is describing meeting his eventual wife. Pass 1-3 missed this; the "young with me" rendering is Whisper degradation of "younger than me" (he later says "when she was 18, I was 22"). Meaning-preserving correction.

pending_context.whisper: "The day was one more or the person."
pending_context.candidate_correction: There was one more — a third person."
pending_context.notes: In the 1967 Claiborne County election candidates discussion. Speaker is enumerating the three Black candidates (Mad Raw, [Ellis] Collins, plus one more). The "day was one more" is Whisper-degraded "there was one more." Worth flagging because the third candidate identification is one of the open adversarial-review items.

pending_context.whisper: "rubbing, and rubbing, I took this as name"
pending_context.candidate_correction: [name forgotten], and [name forgotten], I forget his name
pending_context.notes: Speaker uses "rubbing" as a Whisper placeholder for "[forgot]"/"[blanking on]" multiple times. This appears to be Whisper inventing a filler word for the speaker's audible pauses or "uhhh" hesitations. Catalog pattern.

Speaker references a Navy mate "from Pogilman" who encouraged him to vote. "Pogilman" is severe Whisper degradation; if local-to-Claiborne, "Port Gibson" is the natural fit. If non-local, possibly "Po'gilman" = "Pearl Man" or another MS-town name. Flag for review.

I don't know why I run them all and then had to have a ticket, but, Roman, he wasn't, you know, he, he wasn't a dedicated" reads as if "Roman" might be a SEPARATE NAMED PERSON (a Reverend or local figure who, unlike Dawson, was "not dedicated"). pass9_metadata.credit_formula: match_factor * min(0.10, max(0, 0.02 + 0.001*healed - 0.005*fails)); credit = 0 if healed=0 or match<0.3
